name: runs-root-prepare
description: Shape fixture: the ROOT project ships a `prepare` script that (a) WRITES build output inside the repo (SURFACES as $REPO/prepare-built.txt in escaped_writes), (b) READS a path outside the repo (/etc/hostname -> external_reads), and (c) reads a protected env var (NPM_TOKEN). `npm rebuild --foreground-scripts` / `yarn install --immutable` do NOT run a root `prepare`, so the guest agent runs a SECOND audited pass (`npm run prepare --if-present --foreground-scripts`) to capture it. The root project is not in node_modules, so the agent passes its key via rootPkgKeys (NOT a /work pkgDir) so normalize SURFACES the root fs events instead of throwing or dropping them as $PKG -- forgery-safe (Codex review #1). The `postinstall` gives the main install pass an observable event too.
